videos.description:
Jaipur Tourist Places | Places To Visit In Jaipur | Jaipur Best Places To Visit | Jaipur #jaipur #saturdayshooters #rajasthan 

Jaipur Tour Package  : +91 9034905765

If you're planning a trip to Jaipur, there are plenty of best places to visit in Jaipur that should top your itinerary. Whether you're on a Jaipur trip for 2 days or have 3 days to explore,you'll find that the city offers a mix of history, culture, and stunning architecture. Start with iconic Jaipur tourist places like the Amber Fort, City Palace, and Hawa Mahal, which are among the best places in Jaipur to witness the grandeur of Rajasthan. For those seeking a more in-depth experience, explore Jaipur Rajasthan tourist places such as the Jantar Mantar and Albert Hall Museum. Many Rajasthani travel vlogs offer detailed guides to these tourist places in Jaipur, helping you make the most of your trip. No matter how long your stay, these places to visit in Jaipur will ensure that you experience the vibrant essence of the Pink City!

videos.title: The Ultimate Jaipur Tour Guide: Places To Visit, Things To Do, Forts, Palaces, Markets | Tripoto
videos.description:
If you're planning a trip to Jaipur, aka the Pink City, this guide will make your vacation planning much smoother. From the majestic forts and palaces to the colourful shopping bazaars of Jaipur, we cover it all. 

#Jaipur #TravelGuide #Tourism

Credits: 
Script: Kuntala Banerjee
Narration: Saumia Bhatnagar
Editing: Samik Mukherjee
Produced by: Tripoto Travels

More about Tripoto: 
Some people are born with an addiction to explore the world. They are happiest when they lost in a new city, exploring a hidden path or pushing the boundaries of their comfort zone. They breathe in the deepest when they are surrounded by crisp mountain air and they curl their toes in the pristine sands of peaceful beaches. And it is a tribe of such wanderers that has come together to create a community called Tripoto. 

Tripoto is one of the largest communities of travelers in the world. Travelers post breathtaking photographs, mind-blowing blogs and goosebump-inducing videos on our platforms and share them to inspire fellow travelers. 

Tripoto is essentially your one-stop shop every time you’re looking for itineraries, recommendations and any other travel-related inspiration. Whether it’s the most budget-friendly weekend getaway or a luxurious rejuvenation session you seek, you’ll find it here. Whether you’re looking for the unexplored cafes in the hills or deep-sea diving sites, we have it all. 

That’s not all, going above and beyond recommendations, Tripoto also helps you plan the perfect vacation with our team of partner-agents who customise travel packages to suit your need and offer unmatched prices. Choose from a host of options and pick the one that suits your mood and doesn’t stretch your wallet. 

The best of all things travel are right here - from the desh ki dharti and everything desi to the glitziest destinations abroad - you name it, you can find it on ONE single platform, only on Tripoto. 
We aim to provide you with EVERY SINGLE THING you need to have the best vacation of your life EVERY SINGLE TIME you step out. We’re here to help you make a gazillion happy memories because that’s the only way life is worth living. If you’re not on Tripoto, you’re missing out!

tags: best things to do in jaipur
videos.transcript: Majestic monuments, colourful bazaars, busy streets, and family-run restaurants adorn Jaipur, the capital city of Rajasthan. The city provides an opportunity to explore the remnants of a royal era through its rich food, culture, and warm hospitality. Mesmerising monuments at every corner, bustling markets calling out to shopping enthusiasts and picturesque sunsets welcome the travel enthusiasts in the “Pink City”. Jaipur is a growing city, but it still holds on to its old-world charm. The city offers you a chance to step into the royal past while enjoying the benefits of the modern age. Photographers, nature lovers, art enthusiasts, and history buffs often call Jaipur a second home. Every time you visit this fascinating city, you will be surprised by what you discover. Jaipur is well connected by air, rail, and road. Jaipur has an international airport that has regular flights running to and fro most cities in the country. Since Jaipur is one of the hot-spots for international tourists, you will find easily available trains to the city as well. Jaipur offers a plethora of things to do and places to visit. Here are the top attractions and things to experience while touring the city. Constructed in 1592 and completed in the 8th century by Sawai Jai Singh, the fort is a fine example of Hindu and Rajputana style of architecture. The grand architecture, and the landscaped gardens entertain the visitors with its marvel and grandeur. Most tours begin from the main entrance to the fort which is known as Suraj Pol. The key attractions of the fort are Diwan-e-Aam, Sheesh Mahal, and Sukh Mahal. Jaigarh and Nahargarh Forts are two grand structures that stand tall in the Aravallis. Owing to the proximity of the monuments, both can be covered on the same day when you are exploring Jaipur. Jaipur Fort is also known as the Fort of Victory, its key attraction being Jaivana, the majestic cannon placed at the second level. The architectural feat of the Nahargarh fort is truly mesmerising. Standing tall in the centre of the city is an unmissable part of Jaipur – the City Palace, built by Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh. The architecture of the City Palace is a fusion of three to four architectural styles including Shilpa Shastra, Mughal, European and Rajput.The most attractive highlights of the palace are Chandra Mahal, Diwan-i-Aam and Maharani Mahal. It can take about two to three hours to explore the City Palace. The mesmerising red- and pink-sandstone structure of Hawa Mahal is a treat to the eyes. The mahal has more than 900 small mesh windows which allow cool wind to enter during summer. It allowed the women from the royal family to view the festivities from the windows and participate in the celebrations. Chokhi Dani is a luxury resort located a few kilometers away from the main city. It offers you a peek into the local way of life. The resort recreates the rural villages of Rajasthan and brings together the various art and craft forms that have put the state on the global map. The markets of the city are its lifeline and if you want to experience the pulse of the city, there isn’t a better way. Johari Bazaar is popular for precious stones, semi-precious stones and silver jewellery. Exquisite designs and handcrafted jewellery puts this market on the list of top markets to visit in India. Nehru Bazar is another delight, offering a variety of footwear and bags. Chandpole Market, Kishanpole Market and the Rajasthan Emporium are some other markets to explore in the city. Jaipur is a keeper of traditional recipes and local restaurants that have been serving food lovers for decades. Pyaz kachori, gatte ki sabzi, kadhi, bajra roti, ker sangri, and khichdi are some of the popular dishes that you will find everywhere. Lal Maas is a big temptation and you can't experience the real Jaipur without giving this spicy dish a try. The best budget hotels in Jaipur below INR 3000 are Pearl Palace Heritage, V Resorts Adbhut Jaipur, and Nahargarh Haveli. Some of the mid-range hotels with INR 7000 are Trident Jaipur, Royal Heritage Haveli, and Umaid Bhavan. The Oberoi Rajvilas Jaipur, Taj Rambagh Palace, and Sujan Rajmahal Palace are prominent luxury hotels. For those who desire to stay in a hostel, Zostel is the best option for you. The best time to explore Jaipur is winter. The city shines in a different light and through misty mornings and chilly evenings, you explore a never-before-seen side of Jaipur tourism. The months from October to February make up the peak season in Jaipur. Avoid the summers as the days are extremely hot and this is the least preferred season for travellers. If you happen to travel during the monsoon, we recommend including cycling tours and heritage walks in your itinerary. If you want to explore the city well, the recommendation is to go for 3 days and 2 nights. This will award you sufficient time to visit the city’s iconic monuments, markets, and eateries. The easiest way to explore Jaipur is by hopping on a cycle rickshaw or an auto-rickshaw. Owing to the popularity of Jaipur, private cab companies like Ola and Uber have also made traveling in the city easier. Let us know your favourite haunts in Jaipur in the comments below! For more such detailed guides, hit the subscribe button so that you never miss an update from Tripoto.

safetyNotes: Avoid the summers as the days are extremely hot and this is the least preferred season for travellers

videos.description:
Jaipur is the capital of Rajasthan and is also one of the most popular cities for a traveler both from India and also abroad. It's part of the golden triangle and is a great gateway for someone to start India explorations. 

In today's video, we'll explore some of the top places to visit in Jaipur in two days (two days itinerary). We'll cover history, food, culture and much much more.

videos.title: Your Offbeat 3 Day Jaipur Itinerary | The Solo Female Traveller EP 13 | Curly Tales
videos.description:
You know, I’ve heard so much about Rajasthani culture and hospitality. This is the first time I got to experience it in my first ever visit to Jaipur and all of Rajasthan included.  Jaipur, the pink city has more than what meets the eye. The pink town, pink-effaced buildings, majestic views, and people are what will make me come back to this again and again. 
 
Watch as I explore and dissect some offbeat treks, popular tourist destinations and attractions, and some amazing food to eat. 
 
Until next time, it’s your solo travel guide signing off, 
Neha Nambiar

00:00 - Introduction 
0:30 - Welcome To Jaipur! 
01:24 - Why Jaipur Is Called Pink City 
02:43 - Getting To Jaipur & Stay 
03:38 - Jaipur’s Food & Markets 
04:04 - Touring Jantar Mantar 
04:55 - Hawa Mahal 
06:27 - Offbeat Trek To Hathnikund 
08:04 - Riding Tuk Tuk 
08:43 - The Mysterious Panna Meena 
09:05 - Dancing With Jaipuri Women
10:02 - Exploring The Mighty Amer Fort 
11:20 - Travel Tips Jaipur

videos.transcript: foreign [Music] dude [Music] morning guys it's 6 30 a.m in the morning and i just arrived at jaipur from udaipur after like an 8-hour bus journey i think i'm getting too old for pink city paradise called the pink city it's because in 1876 the prince of wales and queen victoria may india visit welcome [Music] the fastest way to travel to jaipur is via flight to jaipur airport which costs anywhere between five to seven thousand from mumbai jaipur also has a well-connected train network to all the cities of india which cost anywhere between 100 to 2000 rupees depending on where you stay since i was in udaipur i traveled by bus to jaipur which took me 8 hours and cost me 740 rupees i am now checking into mustache hostels unlike udaipur where i stayed in a heritage hotel this time i'll be staying and i'll be going back to my dorms guys i was suggested by many people to stay at moustache hostels in jaipur and so i checked it out and it was one of my best decisions in my entire jaipur trip the hostel is located just a few kilometers away from pink city and has a nice greek touch to its interiors and the best part is that i made amazing friends price starts anywhere between 499 to 3000 per night where you have options to stay in dorms and private rooms [Music] hi so i'm finally heading out on my first day in jaipur the plan today is pretty simple i'm just going to be roaming around all the places that are close by i'll actually show you more than tell you come let's go i hired a rikshaw to take me to pink city and had multiple stops on the way especially the famous eatery shops [Music] urge you to walk through these streets whenever you're here in the past whereby mapping the movements of planets this was built by rajput king savai dressing who was the founder of jaipur city imagine being this advanced back then in the 1900s or whenever this was built really fascinating in 1799 by maharana pratap singh there are many interesting facts about the palace but i'll condense them and name about three one the palace was built for the royal women of rajasthan two it is the world's tallest building without a foundation and three it has no stairs just ramps to go up and down and like i predicted baris anil aghi and so i was stranded here for a while but that didn't stop me from going out and enjoying the rains and that's how i ended my first day in jaipur keeping it light as always [Music] i have so much more to cover and unearth about this beautiful city my total expenditure minus stay was only 900 rupees hi guys good morning it's day two in jaipur aaj ka plan here to go and explore some of the locations like i do in most of my vlogs yesterday was more cultural visit visiting the heritage and today i'm going to be going trekking to this place called hadmikun [Music] one of the many benefits of staying in a hostel is that you make many travel buddies that can give you as a solo traveler more access to explore off-beat and hidden places and i met a lot of them at moustache hey guys we're now going to commence our trek and i am being accompanied with all these wonderful people introduce yourself [Music] 35 minutes away from jaipur city and is on the way to nahadgar kuch trekking many pools of water it's about 45 minutes of trekking one way so best to head here in the i don't know mornings you can see but there are people jumping from a cliff into that pool of water what stood out the most about the place were the uniquely shaped mountain ranges plus the monkeys we were surrounded with a must visit to see a very different side of jaipur [Music] hey guys now that we are done with the trek i'm going to be heading to some of the locations to explore in jaipur and i'm being accompanied by ghanshyam who's the community manager here at mustache they organize amazing offbeat location guided tours of course it's best to do it solo but it's even better to have locals guide you there so are you excited is [Music] unlike other times when i rent a scooter to commute i did something different this is a tuk tuk which is the best way to travel in jaipur it's fun economical and if you're lucky you can get to ride it too guys but i learned how to drive the tuk-tuk on the spot of course it's not normal to do that which is why i actually ended up getting a lot of stairs but that didn't stop me from doing what i wanted to do traveling solo is all about trusting your instincts and having full confidence in yourself to be able to pull off anything and so whenever you get opportunities like these grab it with full confidence [Music] [Music] here they usually visit amir fort but little do people know about panna meenakakund this is a step well built in the 16th century and used to be a famous resting place for travellers saints and more since temperature cafe logue also this place has no entry fee isn't that the best so by venturing around panna meena i found this beautiful temple jahapur i saw some women dancing and i joined along and learned a few steps [Music] [Music] up located about 11 kilometers away from jaipur city ameer fort is a very popular tourist destination is the fort is divided into six courtyards with major attractions like the sheesh mahal or the hall of mirrors which could be illuminated by just one candle [Music] from here it was time for me to head to another famous location or experience radha which was to eat the renowned dal bhati churma of rajasthan and that's how i ended my second day at jaipur hey guys the rahamira suffrage now this is the time i tell you how was my experience here otherwise it was kind of the best time to visit jenwood since it's extremely hot otherwise but a lot of times it was feeling good it helped keep the temperature down hospitality-wise this place was really warm whether it's the people i met on the road whether it is the friends i made and especially the place that i'm staying at mustache they went out of their way to make me feel very comfortable special shout out to gansha who's a community manager here anyone who's heading to mustache must go meet him to have a great time a few more pointers i noticed is that commute here is insane so the best uh travel option here is either tuktuk or riksha it's affordable also two the weather gets really really hot and humid so uh plan your day accordingly except for when it's the monsoons to plan your day accordingly so that's all i can think of thanks for watching the solo female traveler and tuning in on curly days until then it's been a remember wait i'm not done yet i started this episode with showing you my perspective of jaipur and up time had to do the same solo travel is the most emancipating experience you can ever have and if i can do it so can you until next time bye bye my total expenditure for two nights three days was 6666 minus flight charges [Music] [Music] you

would watch I think it's beautiful it's stunning it's an architect just so impressive [Music] [Music] I love this stuff the that was a really good meal at the tattoo Cafe can highly recommend you try it out in the total for two meals and two drinks was 1,300 rupees okay okay they just fled to build the this one okay something I'm finding very interesting here in India is they really love their pigeons like they feed them stucken like bags and bags of seeds and back home we actually despise pigeons like they the Rats of the sky to us so it's very interesting here that they look after their pigeons in arra the guy was telling us all andit he was saying they do a little bit of gambling with the pigeons yes so the pigeons they'll like have their own group of pigeons and then they send them up in the sky sometimes when the pigeons forget where their home is and they land at the other guy's home then that guy gets to keep those pigeons and then he must pay money to the dude that got his pigeons to get them back so that's how they like it's a little bit of gambling in the pigeon industry exploring the roads we seeing a couple of camels here and there which is quite cool apparently rajastan has quite a big camel population and I guess that makes sense since a lot of the Arabians Persians came down from the north into India many years ago yeah this is the city of the cows and the camels they just everywhere they so cute [Music] next stop is the Amber Fort which is apparently an incredibly beautiful place according to Clary yeah according to google inside is actually really good sorry you can't see [Music] photographer no it's okay thank you [Music] life no thank you thank you [Music] see he's such a cute monkey but damn he's naughty and he's missing one arm but he literally just jumped on these motorbikes two of them in a row and like push them over on purpose flipp and naughty [Music] don't suppose that was here in when was this built 159 1599 yeah don't suppose that that's a otherwise it would be almost one of the oldest coffee shops on Earth I'm sure when you come here you're going to be saying no to a lot of things one of the things is Hawkers are everywhere selling all sorts of things number two guides guides guides and more guides everyone wants to be your guide it's up to you if you want to take a guide most of them are you know able to speak English I don't think any other languages yeah there're there and then there's tons of CUO shops on your way out as with most tourist attractions tons of shops to buy things at entrance to the Palace where we are now was 500 rupees per person and we did not choose a guide we just have these boards all over the place and you can just read and see the history for yourself so I won't lie this has been one of the most annoying experiences we've had in India so far people have been harassing us there's been tour guides that have offered us their services and when you say no they have such an attitude and one of them spat on red so I don't know if I can actually recommend this place it's beautiful but it's being ruined by so many of the locals unfortunately it's a real freaking pity I hate when this happens because history is kind of like not even your focus anymore it's more just like getting through the crowds and through the rude people and saying No thank you all the time trying to be so polite and yet they still have the biggest attitudes yeah and there's even signs that say you should complain to somebody about the Hawkers here so then you know it's a problem certainly a beautiful place but unlike the the aggra fort it's not really regulated and looked after especially with regards to who they're letting into the compound and who can be allowed to bother the tourists it's very overwhelming so just be warned um it is a bit of a fiasco so come if you want but it's not at the top of our list of recommendations good experience yeah that's fine it happens of course my guest have a bad experience then I I'm sad oh no don't worry about it don't worry this is done good for like a bad experience about like a uh you know the repetition of the reputation yeah the reputation of Rajan and this is we out of the chaos and being hassled um I just want to say that it is very very beautiful there the I think it was his room that was just covered in mirror work it was so unique I've never seen something like that before and this fort was so big you could tour even more go on and on and on you could spend like 2 to 3 hours there are rate unfortunately we're trying to catch sunset at our next destination so we're like panicking but uh yeah I do think I would recommend it but maybe try and go early early morning when there's not a lot of people there and that way you don't have the hwk is harassing you the mon he's trying to kill you and the locals asking for photos every 2 m so yeah do good and I think it's actually really good to get a guide or a driver that will just be ready to pick you up as soon as you you're done there and take you to the next place it's been so so efficient to have sadic so thank you sadic thank you for your time if you want to maybe use sadik in the future when you are in jaur we will leave his WhatsApp and contact details to his trip advisor page down in the description so you can go check him [Music] out so this is the final stop here in jaur it's nagar Fort um we're at the Viewpoint to be honest we didn't even look at the Fort we just came straight to the Viewpoint to come and see the city and watch the sunset here that's what a lot of people recommend apparently the fort isn't actually um looked after that well and it's not as pretty as Amber Fort so yeah this is what makes it unique it just overlooks the entire city and apparently it used to be like a defense Circle for the entire city of jao that's about all I know about this one [Music] unfortunately that is the sound of hundreds of mosques spread across Jaa during the prayer at 700 p.m. sharp wow it's amazing [Music] love so we are in the fort after all we just quickly popped in off the sun it's it's actually really beautiful in here Stu that's tobacco people have been spitting tobacco in this Monument can you believe it that's going to be the end of today's Adventures in jaur we really enjoyed it here the city has so much beautiful architecture and colors and stuff to offer but we are off to Delhi in the next video going to spend a few days there and then we're off to the north Le and Le du so stay tuned for those videos we'll see you in the next one if you do like it don't forget to give it a like And subscribe goodbye bye [Music] he [Music]

videos.title: Rajasthan Tourist Places | How To Travel in Rajasthan | Rajasthan Itinerary | Rajasthan Travel Guide
videos.description:
Rajasthan has many tourist destination and In this video I will travel to entire rajasthan and cover jaipur, ajmer, pushkar, jodhpur, jaisalmer, udaipur, mount abu, bikaner and many places of rajasthan. I will tell you the budget & itinerary of rajasthan and also share many tips about rajasthan trip. After watching this video you can easily plan your rajasthan trip.

Rajasthan me ghumne ko kafi sari places hai aur is video me mai aapko pura rajasthan ghumaunga aur bataunga ki aapko rajasthan kab kaise aur kaha ghumna chahiye. Sath hi aapko rajasthan ghumne ka kharcha aur rajasthan ghumne ki jankari bhi milegi. Is video ko dekhne ke baad aap apni rajasthan trip ka plan asani se bana paenge.

Jaipur airport complete guide. Travel Tips For First Time Travellers in Hindi. How to travel for the first time in flight. Step by step guide in Hindi. 

#mopaairport #firstflight #goaairport #travelguide #traveltips 

About the video - 

This is a must watch video for those going to travel for the first time in a flight from Jaipur international airport. I have explained the entire process from entering the airport to boarding the flight in simple language. After watching this video you'll know what are the procedures followed at the airport. How to get the boarding pass, How to do the security check at the airport, how to access the airport lounge. Also, I have shared some important information and tips about jaipur airport which may be useful even for the regular flyers.

I took Alliance Air Jaipur to New Delhi flight

videos.title: 5 Reasons NOT to Visit Jaipur in India
videos.description:
Join the journey and SUBSCRIBE to the channel:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC2sLUwm2U3OQziFhFolsRZQ

Follow us on INSTAGRAM for up-to-date info and pictures:  
@Wildvikingtravels
https://www.instagram.com/wildvikingtravels/?hl=ee

and on Facebook: 
https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=100070473976061&show_switched_toast=true

Help keep this channel on the road. 
1) Become a part of the Patreon family:
https://www.patreon.com/wildvikingtravels 
2) Support the travels on Paypal:
paypal.me/wildvikingtravels

See another side of our adventure and check out Liisu's channel:
https://www.youtube.com/user/Mustmaasikas

HELLO THERE:
We are Joe and Liisu, relatively new to YouTube but really excited to be a part of this community. We live small and sustainably in my home on wheels (a lovely van called Freyja)  and from time to time out of our backpacks (we have not named the backpacks yet). On this channel, we ask you to join us on our travel adventures off the beaten track where we show you the beauty of untouched nature and wander into places most tourists don't dare to go.
videos.transcript: this is Jaipur! one of the most visited cities in India. known for its magnificent architecture, and vibrant street life. yet as with many other touristic centers all over the world crazy way to scam foreigners again. there are certain dangers that can ruin one's holiday. so in this video we will tell you five things that will help you decide if this city or even India all together is a place you would like to visit! our adventure today begins about 10 kilometers north of Jaipur in an area where breathtakingly gorgeous historic structures are hidden between the Green Mountain sides friends and to prove our first point we're gonna start by discovering some of the most famous and most visited places here in Jaipur. Amber Palace! Every day tens of thousands of people visit this castle and so one should be ready to wiggle between numerous salesmen offering their goods and services Hi sir! Don't you need a proper guide? No thank you, we don't need a proper guide. and if you manage to get yourself so far without buying a pair of fake sunglasses nor get in a guide you are already a winner the road up to the Amber Palace is steep and made of hundreds of steps. luckily for those not wanting to walk themselves there is also another option. unless you live full time in India I think you're never gonna get used to it and I don't mean the policeman. I mean those guys there. hello! where do you want me to go brother? visiting such old places and walking through its halls and Courtyards has always fascinated me. it's like a window to our past with opportunities to learn about history around every corner yes, we are seeing how toilets look like a thousand years ago and I can guarantee you, they are pretty similar right now. and well sometimes you just have to have fun with it. because even today most of us could not afford a life like this on a Hilltop. she has snakes for hair so I I would feel a connection, we feel a connection. you're not that bad! by now you might wonder why would such places be a reason not to visit this city. but for our point to truly make sense we decided to also show you the second most visited site in Jaipur on our way to the next destination we started to realize why so many people visit this city. it is full of incredible buildings some of which seemingly floating on water. others covered in color give Jaipur its nickname - the pink city! after just a little bit of a walk in the city you could understand why it's called the pink City. walking around here is not always an easy task. crossing the street to Indian style. Yeah, it is only a little bit scary! But as long as you keep on moving it usually works out. Wohoo, still alive! but finally we made it! And the second place every visitor goes to is Hawa Mahal. which means the castle of winds. after a good 15 minutes in the ticket line we were in. once again it was a site where Stones were set on top of each other in quite a unique and beautiful manner. from the top of Hawa Mahal one could take a long and beautiful look on the city. but there was another reason why this place was called the castle of winds and it had to do with the 952 Windows this Palace has. those small windows were for Royal ladies like me to look outside into the street without being noticed. but more than just for looking outside those windows create a constant flow of air in the castle giving it the breeze and making it cool even on the hottest days of the year. after leaving the castle of winds our first point was clear. so as you just saw the two most visited places in Jaipur are old palaces. so if you are not a fan of old architecture maybe Jaipur is not a place for you. but now let's move on! and once again crossing the road Indian style. after spending another hour on the city streets we understood that constant noise is a huge part of the so-called Jaipur experience It's quite hard, wherever we stop for even one second there's gonna be people around us just asking for money but this again is not the reason why you shouldn't visit Jaipur why? because it happens everywhere you'd just have to get used to it. it's not special. but luckily car horns were not the only thing filling the Alleyways. the second reason why you may not like Jaipur has nothing to do with seeing places but more with your nose and your mouth. I have no idea what they're serving I don't actually know what I want but we are staying Indian food is known all over the world but everyone who visits this country should know that street food here is quite different from what we were used to back home in our local Indian restaurant. the biggest difference is that here the food is at least five times spicier. very little spicy yes perfect! and even the no spicy version we ask the vendors can severely burn a Foreigner's mouth wow amazing! this time we were quite fortunate with the amount of spice in our food and of course we also tried the sweeter side of India but eating here made us understand another reason why India might not be for everyone. so as you just saw if you're not gonna try Indian food and you don't like Indian food then maybe Jaipur is not for you. let's see if locals think the same. so if a tourist comes to Jaipur should he still come if he does not like Indian food? um maybe they they didn't find the real taste in Jaipur. so that's why? but in Jaipur we have a very different kind of real taste so don't worry. you can be here and enjoy the street food so it's very delicious food. so pretty much if you don't like Indian food then you haven't tried enough Thank you sir so much, it was delicious. The search for third reason why Jaipur might not be a place to visit for everyone brought us to the Heart of the City. and I'll be honest driving here is no easy task. Cows, elephants and of course thousands of bikes, bicycles and tuk-tuks yet trust me on this one, finding parking can be even more challenging and once we had found the place we got to know another harsh Indian reality. so we just took a parking and of course because we're white they wanted to ask 300 (3.5€) when the rightful price for locals was 50 (0.60€). so that felt a bit harsh we bargained it down to 100 (1.2€) because we have a big car. but that's not the reason not to visit Jaipur because that happens everywhere in India. you're just gonna have to be brave and go with the flow. believe it or not but signs don't have a lot of authority here in Jaipur. After risking our lives a few more times trying to cross the streets we made it to the most vibrant and colorful part of the pink City. Jaipur is known for its numerous shopping opportunities and is often even called the gemstone capital of the world. so all of those people up there they're just selling gemstones that's why that whole crowd was there. opals, rubies, emeralds - you name it and just when you think you have seen it all - A store that only sells Peppers. what happened? the air is spicy! I'm not kidding we were not here for the stones but for something much more precious! a beautiful dress called saree for Liisu. we spent hours strolling the streets and its Little Stores. of course like for most other guys for me it meant finding the most comfortable chair in every store. for Liisu on the other hand it was a hands-on experience where she learned how to tie a saree. a long continuous piece of fabric like a family-sized scarf in a beautiful way. it's really incredible what Indian women can accomplish with only one piece of cloth. if you're not the fan of shopping then Jaipur might not be the best place for you by this time I would say that we had gotten a good feel of the city we had wandered its streets, been a part of its hectic traffic and tried its various tastes but still there were topics we had touched only superficially. so this brings us to the fourth point whether you like it or not you are going to meet a lot of people as India is the most populous country in the world now at least here in Jaipur most locals that we have met either ask us to take one selfie with us or they're trying to sell us something. And to test it all out if all Indian people are the same we decided to put our own skin in the game and today we're gonna meet a total stranger. we were going to meet this person in the evening and the only thing we knew was his name - Ganesh drive to his place took about an hour and I'll be honest the roads at night are even more crazy we saw two car crashes that were both solved by a small yelling contest. as we were getting closer to Ganesh's house the roads are getting darker and smaller. it's a dark alleyway. truthfully, we started to doubt if it was even a good idea. fortunately just before our fear got too serious, we arrived. and as it turns out all this fear was unjustified. Ganesh together with his family was a truly wonderful host we had dinner together which by the way was made non-spicy just for us. and before we could eat Temple and the gods get their share of the food and by every minute we learned more and more about the culture and ways of what it means to be Indian and Hindu. what would you say to people who come here and they don't trust anybody? for other country people they come here - India is not dangerous, you can go with them, you can eat with them, they love you. they try to talk to you, they try to take pictures with you, they'll try to give you food. they want to talk to you. so if you don't want to have any conversations with Indian people maybe Jaipur is not for you. because people here surely are going to approach you but just as Ganesh had said most of them have no ill will and simply are interested in us tourists but also what we heard from him was that there was another side to the city that we had not seen and so on the next day we went on a hunt for the side of Jaipur we couldn't really imagine. part that was silent and peaceful we followed Ganesh's advice and started visiting the holy places of Jaipur. it was stunning, it's amazing just a few steps away from the busy street we're now in Krishna Temple and it's so peaceful, it's so calm. but to really round up the experience we also wanted to see one of Jaipur's most famous temples it was located about five kilometers out of the city. these are some big boys and the drive there was quite different from what it felt driving in the city borders. so peaceful, such nature Don't feel like I'm in Jaipur anymore. of course even here you have to be ready for surprises. crazy way to scam foreigners again. like if you have a cell phone with you they want 150 (2€) extra. it was a problem to us since we usually carry all together around five phones and cameras. The ticket would have cost us seven times more but sadly in places that many tourists visit you are sometimes simply seen as a walking wallet. the temples here are dedicated to one of the main Gods in Hinduism. and this here is Hanuman Temple, the famous monkey temple and you see why and where it gets its name. it was really an amazing time seeing all those monkeys here playing and having fun. and as we were discovering this peaceful side of Jaipur I suddenly realized that the fifth reason not to visit Jaipur might not be as valid as I thought since I wanted to say that if you don't like Hinduism, Jaipur and India might not be a place for you but guess what even if you don't like the religion you can still enjoy watching the monkeys play right

Tripoto is essentially your one-stop shop every time you’re looking for itineraries, recommendations and any other travel-related inspiration
The best of all things travel are right here - from the desh ki dharti and everything desi to the glitziest destinations abroad - you name it, you can find it on ONE single platform, only on Tripoto
We aim to provide you with EVERY SINGLE THING you need to have the best vacation of your life EVERY SINGLE TIME you step out
We’re here to help you make a gazillion happy memories because that’s the only way life is worth living
Here are the top attractions and things to experience while touring the city
Exquisite designs and handcrafted jewellery puts this market on the list of top markets to visit in India
The best budget hotels in Jaipur below INR 3000 are Pearl Palace Heritage, V Resorts Adbhut Jaipur, and Nahargarh Haveli
For those who desire to stay in a hostel, Zostel is the best option for you
The best time to explore Jaipur is winter
If you happen to travel during the monsoon, we recommend including cycling tours and heritage walks in your itinerary
If you want to explore the city well, the recommendation is to go for 3 days and 2 nights
10 BEST Places To Visit In Jaipur | Jaipur City Travel Vlog
In today's video, we'll explore some of the top places to visit in Jaipur in two days (two days itinerary)
